name: BeagleBone Black
description: TI AM3358, classic industrial / education SBC

release-date.comment: Launched April 23, 2013. Rev C (4 GB eMMC, Debian) shipped 2014 and remains the current revision.

status.comment: As of 2026-04-26, Digi-Key shows >1700 units in stock at $80.12 USD; BeagleBoard.org continues to publish official Debian 13.4 IoT images dated 2026-04-23.

open-source-hardware.comment: BeagleBoard.org publishes the full design files (schematics, layout, BOM) on GitHub. The BeagleBone Black Wireless variant carries OSHWA certification; the original BBB shares the same open design lineage.

country-of-origin.comment: BeagleBoard.org Foundation is a US-based non-profit. Boards are typically manufactured/assembled by partners (e.g., Seeed Studio, GHI Electronics) outside the US.

soc.comment: Specific part is AM3358BZCZ100 (1 GHz speed grade).

soc-vendor.comment: Texas Instruments is the silicon designer; the schema's tag set does not include a `ti` tag, so `other` is the closest fit. A future RESEARCH.md tweak could add a `ti` tag (the BeagleBone AI-64, BeaglePlay, and BeagleBone Black all use TI silicon).

cpu-architecture.comment: PRUs (Programmable Real-time Units) are 32-bit RISC cores tightly coupled to I/O — useful for deterministic real-time tasks but not part of the CPU complex.

process-node.comment: AM335x family is fabricated on TI's 65 nm process; not stated explicitly in the BeagleBone docs.

opengl-es-version.comment: PowerVR SGX530 supports OpenGL ES 2.0 (not 3.x). Driver is proprietary and historically fragile under modern kernels.

vulkan-support.comment: SGX530 predates Vulkan; no Vulkan driver available.

hw-video-decode.comment: AM3358 / SGX530 does not have a dedicated video decode block; video is handled by the CPU. No standardized hardware codec support.

ram-max-gb.comment: Single 512 MB DDR3L configuration; not user-upgradable.

ram-type.comment: Datasheet specifies DDR3L (low-voltage DDR3) at 800 MHz. The schema's RAM-type tag set does not include a plain `ddr3` or `ddr3l` tag — `lpddr3` is the closest match. A future RESEARCH.md tweak could add a `ddr3` tag for older boards.

emmc.comment: 4 GB onboard 8-bit eMMC (Rev C); Rev B had 2 GB.

wifi.comment: Original BeagleBone Black has no Wi-Fi. The separate BeagleBone Black Wireless variant adds Wi-Fi 4 + BT, but it is a different product.

bluetooth.comment: No onboard Bluetooth on the original BBB.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: Client/power port is mini-USB-B (USB 2.0), not USB-C.

poe-support.comment: No native PoE on the BBB itself; some third-party capes provide passive PoE handling.

max-display-resolution.comment: Documented max via the onboard NXP TDA19988 HDMI framer.

mipi-dsi.comment: AM3358 exposes parallel LCD signals, not MIPI-DSI; LCD-cape connection uses the cape headers.

mipi-csi.comment: No MIPI-CSI camera connector. Cameras are typically connected via USB or a parallel-camera cape.

audio-jack.comment: No onboard 3.5 mm jack — audio is HDMI-only on the bare board (Wikipedia incorrectly lists a 3.5 mm jack). A 3.5 mm output requires the BeagleBone Audio Cape or similar.

gpio-pins.comment: Two 46-pin cape expansion headers (P8/P9, 92 pins total) carry up to 65 GPIOs along with I²C, SPI, UART, PWM, ADC, and LCD signals. Different from a standard 40-pin RPi header.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: Uses two 46-pin BeagleBone cape headers (P8/P9), not the 40-pin RPi GPIO layout.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Cape ecosystem is BeagleBone-specific, not pin- or mechanically compatible with RPi HATs.

pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: AM3358 SoC has no PCIe controller.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: Multiple I²C, SPI, UART buses available on the cape headers.

power-input.comment: Either a 5.5/2.1 mm 5VDC barrel jack or the mini-USB client port; can also be powered via expansion headers.

idle-power-w.comment: No credible measured-idle figure from a primary review (Geerling/CNX/Phoronix) for the BBB. Vendor docs cite ~210–460 mA depending on activity, which works out to ~1.0–2.3 W at 5 V, but this is not a controlled measurement. Per RESEARCH.md, prefer null over guessing from current draw.

load-power-w.comment: No published full-load wattage from a credible review. SRM lists worst-case current near 460 mA (~2.3 W at 5 V), but this is an upper-bound spec, not a measured load figure.

cooling-required.comment: AM3358 at 1 GHz has ~2 W TDP envelope; the BBB has shipped without any heatsink for a decade with no thermal-throttling reputation.

rtc-battery-header.comment: AM3358 has an internal RTC; the BBB does not bring a battery holder/header to the PCB. RTC time is lost on power-off unless added via a cape.

length-mm.comment: Mechanical doc cites 3.5" × 2.15" (86.36 × 53.34 mm); commonly rounded to 86.4 × 53.3 mm.

form-factor-class.comment: Roughly credit-card sized; not within tolerance of `pi-standard` and uses a custom hole pattern, so the most honest fit is `credit-card`.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Four mounting holes in a BeagleBone-specific layout, not RPi-compatible.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: AM335x has had upstream device-tree support in mainline Linux since the 3.x era; modern kernels (6.12+) boot the BBB with Ethernet, USB, eMMC, and HDMI working out of the box. The PowerVR SGX530 GPU still requires the proprietary userspace blob, which is the main asterisk.

official-os-image.comment: BeagleBoard.org publishes maintained Debian 12 and Debian 13 IoT images, with the latest dated 2026-04-23.

distros-with-images.comment: Official vendor images are Debian-based. Armbian community images and Ubuntu spins are also available. (No board-specific Raspberry Pi OS, by design.)

bootloader.comment: U-Boot, with vendor patches (v2022.04 lineage in current images).

long-term-support.comment: BBB has received continuous OS-image updates from 2013 through 2026 (13 years). Few SBCs match that track record.

android-support.comment: No officially maintained Android image from BeagleBoard.org. Older community Android ports for AM335x exist but are abandoned.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Digi-Key list price as of 2026-04-26 ($80.12). Original 2013 MSRP was $45; price has roughly doubled over the product's lifetime.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Single SKU — only one configuration sold.

available-regions.comment: Stocked at all major global distributors (Digi-Key, Mouser, Element14/Farnell, Adafruit, RS) as of 2026-04-26.

typical-lead-time.comment: Digi-Key shows >1700 units in stock, ships 1–3 days, as of 2026-04-26.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive official docs site with system reference manual, mechanical drawings, schematics on GitHub, and the BeagleBone Cookbook. Among the best in the SBC space.

community-size.comment: Per RESEARCH.md guidelines, BeagleBone is explicitly slotted as `large`.

active-forum-discord.comment: BeagleBoard.org runs an active Discourse forum with regular vendor and community participation.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Mature cape ecosystem (LCD, audio, motor, relay, prototyping, GPS, etc.) plus official cases. Not at RPi scale, but rich and long-lived.
